data.title: [Cleanup] Migrate src/components/lab/ LAB_COLOR (#6B4C9A old purple) to canonical lab palette (#E870A0/#C44878)

data.to:
Discovered during FRAC-24 impl. Four files in src/components/lab/ (DocumentBadge.tsx, TagFilter.tsx, ArchiveSearch.tsx, ArchiveToolbar.tsx) hardcode `const LAB_COLOR = '#6B4C9A'` — the old 'Fractal Bright' purple. The canonical lab palette per FRAC-24 is light=#E870A0 / deep=#C44878. Either update LAB_COLOR to a palette derivation or switch each consumer to import HOUSES and read palette.deep. 

Also from FRAC-21 audit §C6: the references include raw `border-[#6B4C9A]` and `ring-[#6B4C9A]` Tailwind arbitrary classes — replace with token-driven equivalents or move them to a CSS variable.

Not blocking DESIGN.md authoring (lab page bg already canonical). Cosmetic / consistency cleanup.

data.body:
SCOPE REWRITE (from FRAC-19 design decisions session, 2026-06-05).

Original scope: tokenize the Lab purple #6B4C9A as house-publications-accent.

New scope: REMOVE #6B4C9A entirely. It's pre-FRAC-24 palette-migration legacy.
Lab/Publications already has its canonical palette in src/data/houses.ts:
  palette: { light: "#E870A0", deep: "#C44878" }
The 8 raw #6B4C9A sites in src/components/lab/ (TagFilter, DocumentBadge,
ArchiveSearch, ArchiveToolbar, including LAB_COLOR const) must be replaced
with one of the palette values — likely palette.deep for borders/highlights
(better contrast), palette.light for fills/subtle accents. The exact mapping
per site is for FRAC-34's planning agent.

Also drop the legacy `color: "#6B4C9A"` field from the lab entry in houses.ts.

Captured in the FRAC-19 decisions doc:
.lattice/notes/FRAC-19-design-decisions-20260605.md (§ 7).

DESIGN.md (FRAC-19) does NOT declare a house-publications-accent token.

PASS — independent review by claude-opus-4-7-reviewer.

Diff scope: commit c931c71 touches exactly the 4 expected files (ArchiveSearch.tsx, ArchiveToolbar.tsx, DocumentBadge.tsx, TagFilter.tsx). No other files in the PR commit. src/data/houses.ts is NOT touched (as planned — deferred to a follow-up).

Acceptance criteria:
1. grep for #6B4C9A in src/ returns exactly 1 hit (src/data/houses.ts:319, the deferred legacy color field). Zero hits in src/components/lab/*. PASS.
2. grep for LAB_COLOR in src/ returns zero hits. PASS.
3. pnpm tsc --noEmit returns clean (no errors). PASS.
4. pnpm test returns 143 pass / 4 fail, matching the documented baseline (footer Jacquard, navbar abbreviation, neighborhood layout). Zero new failures. PASS.
5. Diff does not touch src/data/houses.ts. PASS.
6. Diff does not touch DESIGN.md, PRD, plan files, or CLAUDE.md. PASS.
7. Every replacement is palette.deep (#C44878). No palette.light slips. PASS.

Pattern verification:
- Two LAB_COLOR const replacements correctly use HOUSES.find by id 'lab' palette.deep with import HOUSES from @/data/houses in both DocumentBadge.tsx and TagFilter.tsx.
- Tailwind arbitrary classes (ring, border, hover border) correctly inline the literal #C44878 since Tailwind cannot consume the runtime const — matches the plan stated rationale.
- All 9 raw-hex sites and all LAB_COLOR const/reference sites migrated. Note: DESIGN.md mentions eight literal sites while plan said nine — the 9th is the second hex on ArchiveSearch.tsx line 45 (focus ring plus focus border in one className), which the diff correctly handles.

DESIGN.md cross-check (Lab/Publications uses palette pinks; the old #6B4C9A is dead): implementation honors the canonical light #E870A0 / deep #C44878 pair. No house-publications-accent or lab-purple token introduced. The houses.ts:319 residual is correctly flagged in DESIGN.md as accepted divergence with a follow-up task.

PRD cross-check (FRAC-22): no product/nav/copy impact. Lab/Publications house identity unchanged.

Visual verification: SKIPPED (no dev server started in this review session).

Verdict: PASS. Moving to done.
